expectations.text: No raw <div> or <span> used as layout containers. Flex, Box, or Card from hearth-react used instead.
expectations.evidence: Component uses Card, CardContent, CardActions, Flex, Divider — all from hearth-react. No raw <div> or <span> present anywhere in the file.

expectations.text: Card (or CardContent / CardActions sub-components) is imported from @utilitywarehouse/hearth-react and used as the outer container.
expectations.evidence: Imports Card, CardContent, CardActions, CardActionLink from @utilitywarehouse/hearth-react. Card is the root element wrapping the whole component.

expectations.text:
All text content uses Hearth typography components (Heading, BodyText, DetailText). No raw

,

-

, or for text.
expectations.evidence: Uses Heading as='h2' for package name, BodyText for labels and cost prefix, DetailText size='2xl' for the monthly cost value. No raw text-rendering HTML elements.

expectations.text: The 'Manage broadband' link uses Link or CardInteraction/CardActionLink from hearth-react. No raw <a> tag.
expectations.evidence: Uses CardActionLink with heading='Manage broadband', leadingIcon, and trailingIcon props. No raw <a> element.

expectations.text: The hub offline warning is conditionally rendered based on a prop, and uses a Hearth feedback component (Alert or similar) rather than a raw element.
expectations.evidence: {isHubOffline && <Alert colorScheme='warning' title='Smart hub offline' text='...' />} — conditional on the isHubOffline prop, using Alert from hearth-react.

expectations.text: A TypeScript interface or type is defined for the component's props, covering at minimum packageName, monthlyCost, contractEndDate, and hubOffline.
expectations.evidence: BroadbandServiceCardProps interface defines: packageName (string), monthlyCost (string), contractEndDate (string), manageHref (string), isHubOffline (boolean, optional).

expectations.text: A BroadbandServiceCard.test.tsx file is present and contains at least one test.
expectations.evidence: BroadbandServiceCard.test.tsx present with 8 tests across 3 describe blocks: 'content rendering', 'hub offline warning', and 'accessibility'.

claims.claim: CardActionLink is used idiomatically as the manage link inside CardActions
claims.evidence: CardActionLink is used with leadingIcon (BroadbandMediumIcon), trailingIcon (ChevronRightSmallIcon), and leadingIconContainerColorScheme='broadband' — this is the correct compound pattern for card actions with icons.

claims.claim: DetailText size='2xl' is used for the monthly cost
claims.evidence: <DetailText size='2xl'>{monthlyCost}</DetailText> — appropriate size for a prominent price display.

suggestions.reason: All assertions check component presence but not semantic quality. No assertion distinguishes between using DetailText (appropriate for a price) vs BodyText or Heading for the cost value. This is a meaningful quality signal the eval misses.
No assertion checks the Card colorScheme prop is set (e.g. 'broadband'), which is a notable with-skill improvement in brand alignment.

eval_feedback.overall: Assertions pass for all meaningful structural requirements. Quality differentiation between runs would benefit from checking prop usage patterns like colorScheme and typography choices for specific content types.
